project_information.project_title: Redwood Grove Community Center Solar Photovoltaic Installation
project_information.project_location_specific: Redwood Grove Community Center, 410 Maple Avenue, Redwood Grove, CA 94955 (APN 017-243-09)
project_information.description_of_nature_purpose_and_benefi: The project consists of installing a 145-kilowatt rooftop solar photovoltaic (PV) system on the existing Redwood Grove Community Center building to offset a substantial portion of the facility’s electricity demand, reduce greenhouse gas emissions, and lower long-term operating costs for the City. The work will be limited to mounting solar panels on the existing roof structure, installing associated inverters and electrical equipment within existing utility rooms, and connecting the system to the existing electrical service without expanding the building footprint or increasing occupancy.

The primary beneficiaries are local residents who use the community center for after-school programs, senior activities, recreation classes, and public meetings, as reduced utility expenditures will allow the City to maintain or expand programming without raising user fees. The project also benefits the broader community by contributing to the City’s adopted Climate Action Plan goals, improving local air quality through reduced reliance on fossil-fuel-generated electricity, and serving as a visible demonstration project that supports public education about renewable energy and sustainability practices.

approval_and_implementation.name_of_public_agency_approving_project: City of Redwood Grove City Council
approval_and_implementation.name_of_person_or_agency_carrying_out_pr: City of Redwood Grove Public Works Department, in coordination with SunPath Solar, Inc.

exemption_status.categorical_exemption_state_type_and_sec: Class 1, Existing Facilities (CEQA Guidelines §15301); and Class 3, New Construction or Conversion of Small Structures (CEQA Guidelines §15303)
exemption_status.reasons_why_project_is_exempt_line_1: The project involves the installation of a small-scale solar energy system on the roof of an existing, fully developed public facility with no expansion of building footprint or change in land use, and is therefore categorically exempt under CEQA Guidelines §15301 (Class 1 – Existing Facilities) and §15303 (Class 3 – New Construction or Conversion of Small Structures). The work is limited to minor alterations to existing electrical and mechanical systems and the addition of rooftop-mounted equipment within an urbanized area with adequate public services and infrastructure.
exemption_status.reasons_why_project_is_exempt_line_2: The project will not increase the capacity or intensity of use of the community center, will not involve ground disturbance beyond minor conduit routing on already paved or landscaped areas, and is not located on a site that is listed as a hazardous waste site or within an environmentally sensitive area. There are no unusual circumstances that would result in a reasonable possibility of a significant environmental effect, and none of the exceptions to the use of categorical exemptions under CEQA Guidelines §15300.2 apply, as there are no cumulative impacts, no significant effects on scenic resources, and no impacts to historical resources or sensitive habitat.

applicant_filing_and_certification.title: Senior Planner, City of Redwood Grove Planning Department
